mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects broad-based economic expansion and improving corporate operating conditions. This leads to higher expected earnings and drives positive equity returns over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production has been in a steady uptrend since the 2009 lows, signaling recovery.
failure_condition: The rank information coefficient between the 6-month change in INDPRO and 20-day forward returns is less than 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) over a 3-month period is a strong, real-time indicator of an impending recession. This triggers a flight to safety and causes a significant sell-off in equities over the following quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:The 'Sahm Rule' formalizes this mechanism as a reliable recession indicator.
failure_condition: In more than 40% of triggered instances, the S&P 500 produces positive 60-day forward returns, indicating the signal is not reliable.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is accelerating while policy rates (FEDFUNDS) remain pinned near zero, real interest rates become sharply negative. This signals a central bank that is 'behind the curve', leading to fears of future aggressive tightening and margin compression, which negatively impacts equity valuations over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:The stagflationary environment of the 1970s demonstrated how this combination is detrimental to equities.
failure_condition: The interaction signal's hit rate for predicting negative returns is below 50% over a 10-year period that includes at least one full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-interest-rate policy (ZIRP) regime, strong growth in retail sales (RSAFS) provides a powerful signal of consumer health and economic momentum. With borrowing costs suppressed, this robust demand translates directly into higher corporate revenues and positive equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:As of Jan 2011, the Fed has maintained ZIRP for over two years, making this regime dominant.
failure_condition: The correlation between RSAFS 3-month growth and S&P 500 20-day forward returns is not statistically positive (p > 0.05) during periods where FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent positive growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) confirms a strengthening labor market, which underpins consumer spending and overall economic activity. This reduces uncertainty and leads to positive equity market performance over the subsequent month.
evidence_refs: fact:The post-2009 recovery in payrolls has been a key focus for investors gauging the strength of the economic rebound.
failure_condition: A 3-month moving average of PAYEMS monthly changes greater than 100k is not followed by a statistically significant positive mean 20-day forward return.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) are a key leading indicator of economic activity due to their large multiplier effects. A sharp acceleration in housing construction signals builder and consumer confidence, predicting broader economic strength and positive equity returns over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:The collapse in housing was central to the 2008 crisis; its recovery is thus a potent signal of normalization.
failure_condition: The average 60-day forward return following a trigger event is not greater than the unconditional average 60-day return over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating year-over-year real GDP growth (GDPC1) provides the most comprehensive confirmation of a durable economic expansion. This reduces macro uncertainty and supports a positive trend in equity markets over a quarterly horizon as earnings forecasts are revised upwards.
evidence_refs: fact:Quarterly GDP releases are benchmark data points that can confirm or deny prevailing market narratives.
failure_condition: Periods where the YoY change in GDPC1 is accelerating do not exhibit a higher mean 60-day forward return than periods where it is decelerating.

mechanism_narrative: A surprisingly high monthly inflation print (CPIAUCSL) causes an immediate negative reaction in equity markets. This is driven by fears of an earlier-than-expected monetary tightening cycle, which would increase discount rates and compress equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:Market participants are highly sensitive to inflation data, especially when monetary policy is very accommodative.
failure_condition: The average 5-day S&P 500 return following a trigger event is not negative and statistically different from zero (t-test p > 0.05).
